events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the inspection conclusion directly to `/app/answer.json`. The observed AAV prohibition is a code-compliance issue at rough-complete, so I’m treating it as a failed installed condition.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"medium\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 80,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.9,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Air admittance valve installed where prohibited by applicable IPC/UPC requirements or local amendments\",
    \"Trap venting method is non-compliant for the reviewed location\",
    \"Rough-in should not be covered or accepted until an approved venting arrangement is installed\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop closeout for this rough-in area\",
    \"Remove the prohibited AAV or revise the venting to an approved atmospheric vent connection\",
    \"Verify the corrected trap and vent layout against IPC/UPC and local authority requirements before inspection signoff\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The component involved is an air admittance valve serving trap venting. Because the observed condition is an AAV installed in a location where it is prohibited, the rough-complete work is installed but defective and non-compliant; it requires correction before cover or acceptance.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"IPC air admittance valve provisions and limitations\",
    \"UPC venting requirements and local AAV prohibitions or amendments\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json && cat /app/answer.json"
